_deterministic: Pipeline is deterministic. Repeated runs produce identical results.
GD-ALL-INF: All 4 input guards passed. Data is valid.
GD-D2M-ERR: Rejected: D must be ≥ 2 (simplex requires at least 2 parts).
GD-N5M-ERR: Rejected: N must be ≥ 5 (insufficient data for variance trajectory).
GD-NAN-ERR: Rejected: NaN values detected in data.
GD-INF-ERR: Rejected: Inf values detected in data.
S4-CLO-INF: Simplex closure successful. All rows sum to 1.
S4-ZRN-INF: No zero replacement needed. All values positive.
S5-CLR-INF: CLR transform complete. All rows sum to zero.
S6-ZRO-WRN: Zero variance detected. Data may be constant.
S7-BWL-INF: HVLD: bowl shape — variance accelerating (system integrating).
S7-HIL-INF: HVLD: hill shape — variance decelerating (system segregating).
S7-LRQ-WRN: HVLD R² < 0.5 — poor fit, trajectory may be non-polynomial.
S8-NAT-INF: Classification: NATURAL — transcendental match within δ < 1%.
S8-INV-WRN: Classification: INVESTIGATE — nearest match δ between 1% and 5%.
S8-EUL-DIS: Euler-family constant detected. Pipeline geometry resonates with system structure.
S8-TGT-DIS: Precision match: δ < 0.001 — structural resonance at high precision.
S9-EIT-INF: EITT entropy invariance: PASS at all compression ratios.
S9-EIF-WRN: EITT invariance: FAIL at one or more compression ratios.
S9-STL-DIS: Stalls detected — entropy rate dropped below threshold.
S9-SPK-DIS: Spikes detected — entropy rate exceeded threshold.
XU-PCD-DIS: Carrier dominance: one carrier contributes >60% of variance.
XC-TEF-DIS: Dominant directed information flow identified between carriers.
XC-TEZ-CAL: Transfer entropy is zero — system is compositionally memoryless.
XC-PIR-DIS: PID: carriers share redundant information — coupled dynamics.
XC-PIU-DIS: PID: unique carrier information — independent dynamics.
XC-RPS-DIS: Stable pairwise ratio — these carriers are compositionally locked.
XC-RPV-DIS: Volatile pairwise ratio — these carriers are independent.
XC-ZCR-DIS: Zero-crossing events — compositions approaching simplex boundary.
SM-BPO-DIS: Bimodal population detected. Investigate: decompose by subpopulation or add a distinguishing carrier.
SM-SCG-INF: Smooth convergence — single population evolving continuously. Expected mode for well-sampled natural systems.
SM-MCA-WRN: Missing carrier suspected. A physical channel may be untracked. Add it and re-run.
SM-DMR-DIS: Domain resonance — Euler-family geometry encodes deep structure. Compare with other decompositions in this domain.
SM-CPL-DIS: Carrier coupling — carriers share a common physical driver. The stable ratio encodes a constraint.
SM-IND-DIS: Carrier independence — each carrier governed by a separate mechanism. Decomposition may be maximally informative.
SM-DGN-WRN: Degenerate simplex — data approaches boundary. Check if zero values are physical or artefactual.
SM-RTR-DIS: Regime transition detected. Stall points mark boundaries. Consider splitting data at the transition.
SM-TNT-DIS: Turbulent but natural — real complexity that decimation destroys. The oscillation pattern is diagnostic.
SM-OVC-CAL: Overconstrained — near-perfect fit. Verify this is measured data, not model output.
